items.title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI

items.title: Amazon is already offering new OpenAI products on AWS
items.description: A day after OpenAI got Microsoft to agree to end exclusive rights, AWS announced a slate of OpenAI model offerings, including a new agent service.

items.title: Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages
items.description: Amazon's new "Join the chat" feature lets you ask questions about products and receive AI-powered audio responses.

items.title: Match Group invests $100M in Sniffies, a cruising app for gay men
items.description: The app is Match Group's newest attempt to get mobile users excited about online romance again.

items.title: Google expands Pentagon’s access to its AI after Anthropic’s refusal
items.description: After Anthropic refused to allow the DoD to use its AI for domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons, Google has signed a new contract with the department.

items.title: Paragon is not collaborating with Italian authorities probing spyware attacks, report says
items.description: Despite promising to help determine what happened with the hacks targeting journalists and activists in Italy, Israeli American spyware maker Paragon has reportedly not responded to authorities’ requests for information.

items.title: US Supreme Court appears split over controversial use of ‘geofence’ search warrants
items.description: The U.S. top court is expected to rule on whether to allow police to identify criminal suspects by dragnet searching the databases of tech giants.

items.title: Australia forces Big Tech firms to pay for news or face a 2.25% tax
items.description: The more deals platforms make with media outlets, the less they pay. If enough agreements go through, that effective rate drops to 1.5%, which could generate between A$200 million and A$250 million back into Australian journalism.

items.title: Lovable launches its vibe-coding app on iOS and Android
items.description: The app allows developers to vibe code web apps and websites on the go.

items.title: Founder of Shark Tank-backed startup Scholly sues his acquirer Sallie Mae
items.description: Chris Gray is suing his startup’s acquirer, Sallie Mae, for wrongful termination and alleging it's selling student data through a subsidiary. Sallie Mae denies the allegations and vows to fight.

items.title: Apple introduces a cheaper option for App Store subscriptions
items.description: Apple is adding a new subscription option that lets app developers offer lower monthly pricing in exchange for a 12-month commitment.

items.title: Snapchat brings AI-powered conversational advertising to its app
items.description: Users will be able to chat with a brand's AI agent to do things like ask questions and get recommendations.

items.title: YouTube is testing an AI-powered search feature that shows guided answers
items.description: YouTube is rolling out the new AI search feature to Premium subscribers in the U.S. on an opt-in basis.

items.title: BCI startup Neurable looks to license its ‘mind-reading’ tech for consumer wearables
items.description: The startup specializes in "non-invasive" "mind-reading" tech — a kind of neural data collection that, its CEO hopes, will have all sorts of consumer applications.

items.title: Red Hat’s OpenClaw maintainer just made enterprise Claw deployments a lot safer
items.description: Tank OS puts OpenClaw AI agents into a container that lets it run reliably and more safely, especially for those running fleets of them.

items.title: Otter’s new feature lets users search across their enterprise tools
items.description: With this launch, users can connect their Gmail, Google Drive, Notion, Jira, and Salesforce accounts and query that data along with existing meeting data. The company said that it will soon allow connections with Microsoft Outlook, Teams, SharePoint,

items.title: Drizzle on top: a new high-end dog food brand is coming for the 1%
items.description: Golden Child is launching with two "five-star" products: a fresh frozen meal system and, more intriguingly, a "drizzle." It has also raised $37 million in funding.

items.title: India’s Snabbit closes $56M round as investor interest in on-demand home services heats up
items.description: Snabbit now processes over 40,000 daily jobs and has cut costs sharply as it expands across cities and services.

items.title: Letterboxd, the social platform for film buffs, reportedly looking for new owner
items.description: Potential buyers of Letterboxd include Versant, the parent company of CNBC and MS NOW, and Hollywood media company The Ankler, according to Semafor.

items.title: Consumers lost $2.1B to social media scams in 2025, FTC reports
items.description: The agency reports that losses from social media scams have increased eightfold and that social media scams resulted in higher losses than any other method scammers used to contact consumers.

items.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
items.description: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.
